name: GitHub Projects API (GraphQL)
what_it_does: GitHub Projects v2 is a flexible project management tool built on top of GitHub issues and pull requests. The GraphQL API enables agents to create and manage project boards, add items (issues/PRs/draft items), update custom fields, set status, and automate project workflows.
use_cases: Automatically triaging and adding issues to project boards
Updating project item status based on CI/CD pipeline outcomes
Creating draft items for agent-identified work or technical debt
Reporting on project status and velocity across sprints
Syncing project state with external planning tools
not_for: Non-GitHub workflows — Projects v2 is tightly coupled to GitHub issues and PRs
Teams not already using GitHub for code hosting
best_when: An agent is already working within the GitHub ecosystem and needs to manage work items alongside code.
avoid_when: Your team uses Jira, Linear, or another issue tracker as the source of truth — syncing between systems adds fragility.

auth.notes: GitHub Apps are strongly recommended for agents — fine-grained permissions per repo/org, no user token expiry, and installation-level tokens. PATs (classic and fine-grained) also work. OAuth apps for multi-user scenarios. Projects v2 requires 'project' scope on classic PATs or specific GraphQL mutations granted to GitHub Apps.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Unlimited projects on Free/Pro plans; unlimited collaborators
pricing.paid_tiers: Included in GitHub Free, Pro ($4/mo), Team ($4/user/mo), Enterprise (custom)
pricing.notes: Projects v2 is included with all GitHub plans at no additional cost. API access is part of GitHub's standard API offering.

performance.rate_limits: 5000 points/hour for authenticated users; GraphQL queries cost variable points based on complexity

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: GraphQL errors are returned in the errors array with message and path. GitHub-specific error codes are sometimes vague. Rate limit errors include remaining points and reset time in headers which is useful for agents.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: GraphQL mutations are not idempotent by default. Adding an item that is already in a project returns the existing item rather than duplicating — a useful behavior. Creating draft items always creates a new one.
known_agent_gotchas: Projects v2 is GraphQL-only — no REST fallback; agents must handle GraphQL query construction
Rate limiting uses a point system, not a simple request count — complex queries cost more points
Node IDs for projects use base64-encoded global IDs; store them, do not construct them
Custom fields must be queried by field ID, not field name — fetch field metadata first
Draft items do not have associated GitHub issues; mutations differ from issue-backed items
Organization-level projects require org admin token or GitHub App with org-level installation
Project item ordering is manual/human-driven — no API for setting sort position

score_rationale: GitHub Projects v2 GraphQL API is well-integrated with the GitHub ecosystem and has strong auth options (GitHub Apps), webhooks, and cursor pagination. The GraphQL-only interface and point-based rate limiting add complexity for agents. Works best when the agent is already using GitHub APIs for code operations and projects management is an incremental capability. Not worth the integration cost if you are not already on GitHub.
